target_notes: Clozapine — atypical antipsychotic (Clozaril). Has a black-box warning for agranulocytosis, myocarditis, seizures, orthostatic hypotension, and constipation; only used for treatment-resistant schizophrenia. The chosen primaryid is a 30-year-old male EU patient hospitalized after multiple psychiatric reactions while on clozapine 125 mg/day. PS=primary suspect. caseversion=3 means the report was updated twice after initial submission.
capture_method: Downloaded the 73 MB FAERS 2025Q3 quarterly ZIP via curl from https://fis.fda.gov/content/Exports/. Extracted the 7 $-delimited ASCII tables (DEMO, DRUG, REAC, OUTC, RPSR, THER, INDI) to /tmp scratch (uncompressed ~430 MB). Used Polars to (a) scan DRUG for rows where drugname contains 'CLOZAPINE' and role_cod = 'PS' (primary suspect), (b) join to OUTC for serious outcomes (DE/LT/HO), (c) pick the primaryid with the highest reaction count among those. Filtered every table to that primaryid, wrote each subset to inputs/extracted/<table>.txt. Deleted /tmp scratch. Persisted footprint: ~3 KB.
